title: Pleiocardia orbiculata
intro: Pleiocardia orbiculata, also known as the "round-leaf" species, is a type of plant that belongs to the family Bivalvia, specifically a genus of clams, but more commonly referred to as a type of medicinal plant in traditional herbalism. It is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as cardiovascular disease and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Pleiocardia orbiculata include glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pleiocardia orbiculata is often prepared as a decoction or extract, and is commonly used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Pleiocardia orbiculata is a species of saltwater clam in the family Veneridae. It belongs to the kingdom Animalia and the phylum Mollusca. The class of this species is Bivalvia, which is a group of clams and mussels. The order Veneroida also falls under the classification of Pleiocardia orbiculata, further specifying its type of clam. This classification helps scientists understand its characteristics and behaviors within the marine ecosystem.
uses: Pleiocardia orbiculata, a marine bivalve mollusk, has been found to possess various medicinal properties. Its extracts have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. Some research suggests that compounds isolated from Pleiocardia orbiculata may exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral properties. However, more comprehensive studies are needed to fully understand its medicinal applications and potential therapeutic uses. Further research is required to unlock the full potential of Pleiocardia orbiculata as a source of bioactive compounds with medicinal value.
benefits: Pleiocardia orbiculata has been studied for its potential health benefits, particularly in traditional medicine. It has been reported to have antibacterial properties, which may help in preventing infections. Additionally, it is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which could aid in reducing swelling and pain. Its antioxidant properties may also help in protecting against cell damage and oxidative stress. Further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential applications in human health.
parts: Pleiocardia orbiculata.  This species of clamshell is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia. In some cultures, the animal's organs are used to treat various health issues, such as fever and digestive problems.
